description:
No spec subject

From critical review of specled_-155 branch (consolidated report: ~/vaults/rmbrs-vault/code_review/critical/specled_ex/2026-07-23/specled_-155-aggregate-first-spec-coverage/20260723-161424-consolidated.md, review_commit af16144; raw finding TEST-H1, calibrated M).

test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs:225 decode_coverdata/1 calls :cover.stop/0 + :cover.start/0 in the HOST test BEAM (line 227), tearing down the coverage coordinator any outer 'mix test --cover' run depends on. This corrupts specled's own coverage measurement (mix.exs:10 configures test_coverage threshold: 90) and violates the child-BEAM quarantine discipline documented in aggregate_test.exs's own moduledoc.

Fix: move the :cover decode work into a child BEAM (reuse the existing child-BEAM ingest pattern from aggregate_test.exs), so the host suite's cover server is never stopped. Verify by running 'mix test --cover' and confirming the summary is sane / suite passes both with and without --cover.

comments.text:
ADMISSION (specled_-155 epic policy):
Allowed touches: test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs; .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md (scenario co-update only)
Out of Scope (files): lib/**, .github/**, docs/**, all other test files
Out of Scope (intent): no new deps; no behavior change to mix spec.cover.test itself; no changes to snapshot_test.exs's additive cover usage (verified safe — tolerates already_started, never stops)
Verification: (1) mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs --include integration green; (2) same file under --cover produces a real per-module tally (host :cover server survives decode_coverdata) — counterfactual on epic branch af16144: vacuous 100.00% total with zero module rows; (3) mix spec.check --base specled_-155/aggregate-first-spec-coverage exit 0.

IMPLEMENTED
Session: 9355be03-5df8-4ea9-b29e-3ee9a1cd4dcc
Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mzubieta-dev-mz-specled-ex/9355be03-5df8-4ea9-b29e-3ee9a1cd4dcc.jsonl

Commit: bf1a7b3 (branch specled_-aav/decode-child-beam, base af16144, worktree /Users/mzubieta/dev/mz/specled_ex-decode-child-beam)
Files touched: test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs, .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md
Tests added: none new — rewrote private helper decode_coverdata/1 (child-BEAM quarantine); the covering test remains the existing cumulative-parity tripwire, assertions unchanged
Verification stubs flipped: none
Verification tier: change-scoped — mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs --include integration (8/8); same file under --cover (real per-module tally, host cover server survives; epic-branch counterfactual shows vacuous 100.00%/zero-module summary); mix spec.check --base specled_-155/aggregate-first-spec-coverage (0 findings); mix format --check-formatted. Full-system proof deferred to the epic merge gate / PR CI
Final-tier runs: 1

Summary: decode_coverdata/1 no longer stops/starts the host BEAM's :cover server; the decode now runs in a child elixir -e process and returns sorted per-module per-line call counts as Base64-encoded ETF between output markers (immune to :cover.import stdout warnings). The cumulative_parity_tripwire scenario's when-clause is co-updated to pin the child-BEAM decode discipline, clearing branch_guard_missing_subject_update honestly rather than via trailer.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: subagent verifier-aav
Log: n/a — subagent

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 2 (.spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md, test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (no lib/**, .github/**, docs/**, or other test files touched)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 0 flipped by this ticket (spec-verification block byte-unchanged in diff; all entries were already execute: true — confirmed none needed flipping). Spec change is scenario co-update only: cumulative_parity_tripwire when-clause now pins child-BEAM decode.
- Requirements coverage:
  - specled.coverage_capture.cumulative_parity: covered by test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs:178 (assertion at line 211: decode_coverdata(plain) == decode_coverdata(per_test)). Would fail if arming the --per-test formatter perturbed the cumulative per-module/per-line totals mix test --cover exports. Seams: none — real scaffolded child-BEAM fixture + real elixir -e subprocess decode; no Mock/Memory/fake/stub/injected doubles.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none — no mix.exs change (no new deps), no lib change (no behavior change to mix spec.cover.test), snapshot_test.exs untouched.
- Targeted verification: PASS.
  - mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs --include integration => 8 tests, 0 failures.
  - same file with --cover => 8 tests, 0 failures AND a real per-module tally (e.g. SpecLedEx.Coverage.Store 24.44%, SpecLedEx.Config.TestTags 100.00%), proving the host :cover server survives decode_coverdata. Exit 3 is coverage-threshold-not-met (only one file ran) — expected, not a defect.
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design).

Verdict: PASS

Notes: Advisory only — the tripwire's equality assertion has a pre-existing theoretical vacuous-pass surface (if both decodes returned []), but a failed child decode raises on the marker split (lines 256-257) rather than silently returning [], and the direct --cover run independently proves non-vacuous per-module tallies. Consistent with the never-:cover.reset / child-BEAM-quarantine invariant in specled.decision.aggregate_first_spec_coverage. Not introduced or worsened by this ticket.

TEST_CRITIC: FIXED (iteration 1)
Critic session: subagent test-critic-aav (elixir-test-critic)
Blocking findings: 1 (resolved: 1, waived: 0)
Files reviewed: test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs

Blocking: parity assertion vacuously green when both decodes return [] — fixed in bef5400 with a non-emptiness floor plus a fixture-module (Covered) presence assertion before the equality check. Advisory (also applied): marker-presence guard before String.split so a marker-less zero-exit child run fails with the child output visible. Advisory (no change needed): elixir-on-PATH portability is on par with the existing run_fixture_mix_test/2 shell-out.

IMPLEMENTED (updated)
Commit: bef5400 (supersedes bf1a7b3 record; branch specled_-aav/decode-child-beam, base af16144)
Files touched: test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs, .spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md
Verification tier: change-scoped — file tests 8/8 with --include integration post-fix; mix spec.check --base specled_-155/aggregate-first-spec-coverage clean; format clean
Final-tier runs: 2 (finals_gt1 — second run triggered by test-critic blocking finding)

VERIFY: PASS (re-verified at HEAD bef5400)
Session: subagent verifier-aav
Log: n/a — subagent

Covers commits bf1a7b3 + bef5400 (base af16144). bef5400 is test-only, resolving the vacuous-pass surface noted in the prior VERIFY.

Mechanical checks (unchanged):
- Files touched: 2 (.spec/specs/coverage_capture.spec.md, test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0
- mix.exs untouched (no new deps); no lib/**, .github/**, docs/**, or other test files.

Delta introduced by bef5400 (all in the allowed test file, cumulative-parity tripwire):
- Non-emptiness floor: asserts plain_counts != [] before the parity equality, so two empty decodes can no longer pass vacuously.
- Covered-module presence: asserts the decoded totals include the fixture's Covered module (defmodule Covered at line 373, exercised via hello/0 + add/2) — tuple pattern {{mod, _line}, _count} matches :cover.analyse(_, :calls, :line) output.
- Marker-presence guard: asserts output has both DECODE begin/end markers before String.split, converting a marker-less child decode into a clear failure rather than a MatchError.

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs flipped by this ticket: 0 (spec-verification block byte-unchanged across both commits).
- specled.coverage_capture.cumulative_parity: covered by test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs:178 (parity equality now at line 220, guarded by the non-emptiness + Covered-presence floors at lines 214-219). Would fail if arming the --per-test formatter perturbed cumulative per-module/per-line totals, OR — new — if the decode silently returned empty/Covered-less content. Seams: none (real scaffolded child-BEAM fixture + real elixir -e subprocess; no mocks/stubs).
- Intent-level scope concerns: none.
- Targeted verification: PASS — mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_cover_test_test.exs --include integration => 8 tests, 0 failures at HEAD bef5400.
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design).

Verdict: PASS

Notes: The advisory vacuous-pass Note from the prior VERIFY is now closed by bef5400. No new concerns.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit)

Log: ~/.claude/projects/-Users-mzubieta-dev-mz-specled-ex/9355be03-5df8-4ea9-b29e-3ee9a1cd4dcc.jsonl

scope violations, passing targeted verification, complete seam disclosure, and
no seam substituting production wiring. TEST_CRITIC: FIXED (1 blocking resolved, 0 waived). No auditor was spawned per policy.

APPROVED. Merged bef5400 into specled_-155/aggregate-first-spec-coverage via --ff-only (epic now at bef5400); make smoke green; stage worktree specled_ex-decode-child-beam cleaned up. Closing per auto-audit sign-off.

title: Fix decode_coverdata host :cover teardown in spec_cover_test_test.exs (run in child BEAM)
